sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: Added new ANNOTATION config section with boolean setting pe_auto_expand_annotation (default: true). When enabled, expandAnnotation() clicks the 'Show more…' anchor (div.annotation p a.annotation-toggle) on page load, revealing the full annotation text without requiring a manual click. Runs as STEP 1/3 in the execution block, before section toggling, so the annotation body is already expanded when initPageHeaders() constructs the collapsible wrappers.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Fixed tracklist content being cut off on large releases (e.g. multi-LP box sets). Root cause: applyGalleryState() and the initial wrapper/gallery state both used a hard-coded max-height of 5000 px for the expanded state. On a 5-LP release the tracklist content exceeds that limit and the overflow is hidden, so the last medium's tracks were invisible. Fix: (1) initial expanded wrappers now carry no max-height constraint (maxHeight: ''); (2) when expanding, applyGalleryState() reads the element's actual scrollHeight and animates from 0 to that value, then clears max-height on transitionend so the section can resize freely; (3) when collapsing from an unconstrained state, the function snapshots scrollHeight and forces a reflow (void el.offsetHeight) before animating to 0, giving the browser a concrete start value for the CSS transition.

Fixed annotation section: (1) The 'Annotation' h2 was never made collapsible because div.annotation-body (its first sibling) contains a Markdown-rendered h2 ('Barcode and Other Identifiers') that triggered the old sibling-collection stop guard (el.querySelector('h2')), leaving the annotation h2 with no collected siblings. (2) That same annotation-content h2 was incorrectly enhanced as an independent collapsible section header. Root cause: querySelectorAll('h2') collected all h2 elements including Markdown headings inside div.annotation-body, and the sibling-stop guard halted at any descendant h2 rather than only at candidate section-header h2s. Fix: added .filter(h2 => !h2.closest('.annotation-body')) to exclude annotation prose h2s from the candidate list; changed the sibling-collection loop guard from !el.querySelector('h2') to !nativeH2s.some(nh2 => el.contains(nh2)) so it only stops at elements that contain a genuine candidate section header. Result: 'Annotation' h2 is correctly made collapsible (its siblings div.annotation-body, 'Show more' link, and annotation-details are all included), and annotation-body content h2s are never touched.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Fixed event-bubbling clash with GenerateRecordingCommentForRelease (and any other userscript that appends interactive controls inside a native MB h2). Root cause: attachHeaderBehavior() installed a click listener on the h2 element; because the 'Edit recording comments' button is appended *inside* h2.tracklist via .appendTo($header), clicking it caused the click event to bubble up to the h2 and trigger the collapse/expand logic, which hid the wrapper div containing the tracklist table. Fix: added an early-return guard at the top of the h2 click handler — if the event originated from an interactive descendant (button, a, input, select, or textarea) and the event target is not the h2 itself, the handler returns immediately without toggling the section.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Fixed a bug in initPageHeaders() where clicking a section header (e.g. 'Tracklist') would also collapse a logically independent sibling section (e.g. 'Credits') whose h2 lived inside a wrapper div rather than directly in the same parent. Root cause: the sibling-collection while-loop only stopped at a direct h2 sibling (el.tagName === 'H2'), so a wrapper element such as div#bottom-credits — which contains its own h2 — was swept wholesale into the outer section's collapsible div. Fix: extended the loop guard to also stop when the current sibling contains a descendant h2 (!el.querySelector('h2')), ensuring wrapper elements that house their own section headers are never included in another section's collapsible group and remain independently collapsible.

sections.label: ⚙️ Internal
items: Added visual BEGIN/END block comments around every function group that implements a specific configuration section. The three groups are now each enclosed in matching '// BEGIN: <SECTION NAME>' / '// END: <SECTION NAME>' banner comments: SHARED UTILITIES (isOverviewTabActive, ART_HEADER_CLASS / ART_GALLERY_CLASS constants), SECTION TOGGLING (makeTooltip, applyGalleryState, attachHeaderBehavior, initPageHeaders — controlled by pe_enable_section_toggling), and CAA / EAA ILLUSTRATED DISCOGRAPHY (displayArtGallery — controlled by pe_enable_caa_eaa / pe_image_size / pe_collapsed_by_default). Each banner also lists the config keys and function names it owns.

Reordered functions to match logical config-section grouping: initPageHeaders() moved before displayArtGallery() so that all SECTION TOGGLING functions (makeTooltip, applyGalleryState, attachHeaderBehavior, initPageHeaders) are contiguous within their BEGIN/END block, followed by the CAA / EAA ILLUSTRATED DISCOGRAPHY block (displayArtGallery).

Labeled the two execution steps in the main dispatch block with 'STEP 1/2 — SECTION TOGGLING' and 'STEP 2/2 — CAA / EAA ILLUSTRATED DISCOGRAPHY' inline comments, making it immediately clear which feature is running at each point and repeating the relevant feature-flag guard for quick reference.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Fixed indentation of the pe_enable_debug_logging return statement inside the VZ_MBLibrary constructor callback — the line was two levels under-indented relative to the surrounding arrow function body.

Fixed inaccurate performance timer: Lib.time(timerLabel) / Lib.timeEnd(timerLabel) previously bracketed only the synchronous Promise creation of displayArtGallery(), not the actual async fetch+render work, so the timer always reported ~0 ms. The execution block now wraps the call in a small async IIFE and awaits displayArtGallery(), so Lib.timeEnd() fires only after the full gallery render cycle completes.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Renamed all configSchema keys from the generic 'ca_' prefix to 'pe_' (Page Enhancer) to better reflect the script's identity and avoid potential key collisions with other userscripts sharing the same VZ_MBLibrary storage backend. Affected keys: ca_enable_debug_logging → pe_enable_debug_logging, ca_image_size → pe_image_size, ca_collapsed_by_default → pe_collapsed_by_default.

Added new boolean setting 'pe_enable_caa_eaa' (default: true) in the CAA/EAA ILLUSTRATED DISCOGRAPHY config section. When set to false, the entire CAA/EAA art gallery feature is skipped at runtime without any DOM changes.

Added new config section 'SECTION TOGGLING' with boolean setting 'pe_enable_section_toggling' (default: true). When set to false, the collapsible h2 header behaviour is completely disabled and initPageHeaders() exits immediately.

Added comprehensive debug/warning logging with icons/glyphs throughout all major code paths: startup (🚀), MBID/DOM detection (🔎), feature-flag guards (🚫), HTTP fetch lifecycle (🌐📡📦), gallery DOM construction (🖼️🏷️🔽🔼), per-image rendering details, section toggling events (🖱️), skipped/empty state warnings (⚠️), and success confirmations (✅). All debug messages are gated behind pe_enable_debug_logging and routed through Lib.debug()/Lib.warn()/Lib.error().

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: ChangeLog entry missing from Tampermonkey browser menu — root cause: VZ_MBLibrary.fetchRemoteText() uses GM_xmlhttpRequest to fetch the remote changelog JSON, but ReleasePageEnhancer did not declare @grant GM_xmlhttpRequest in its userscript header. Without that grant GM_xmlhttpRequest is undefined at runtime; fetchCachedText() therefore returned {data:null} immediately, initRemoteContent() exited early, and GM_registerMenuCommand('📜 ChangeLog', …) was never reached. Fix: added @grant GM_xmlhttpRequest to the ==UserScript== header. ShowAllEntityData already carried this grant (which is why its ChangeLog menu entry worked).

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Extended collapsible header behaviour (light-orange background, Click to toggle, Ctrl+Click to toggle all) to all native MusicBrainz h2 section headers on the page (e.g. 'Track listing', 'Additional details', 'Relationships'), not just the script-added CAA/EAA art gallery headers. Each h2's sibling content is wrapped in a collapsible div. The new shared attachHeaderBehavior() helper is called by both initPageHeaders() and displayArtGallery(), keeping the implementation DRY.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Enhanced gallery headers: archive-aware tooltips now read 'Click to show/hide CAA Art Images' or 'Click to show/hide EAA Art Images' (two-line tooltip, second line explains Ctrl+Click). Added light-orange background (#FFE4B5 / moccasin), padding, and border-radius to all art gallery h2 headers. Ctrl+Click on any art gallery header now collapses or expands all script-managed galleries at once.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Added event art support for MusicBrainz event pages. On event pages, artwork is now fetched from the Event Art Archive (eventartarchive.org/event/<mbid>) instead of the Cover Art Archive. The displayArtGallery() function is generalised with archiveUrl and artLabel parameters; the execution block detects the page type (release vs. event) and passes the correct archive URL, human-readable label ('Cover art' / 'Event art'), and short acronym ('CAA' / 'EAA') accordingly.

sections.label: ⚙️ Internal
items: Refactored settings, logging and changelog handling to an external shared library (VZ_MBLibrary).

sections.label: 🎉 Release
items: First official release version.
